title: Why Your Soccer Betting Model Isn’t Profitable
usable_claim: Picking winners or high accuracy is not enough; a betting model needs calibrated probabilities and positive expected value after price/overround.
vmax_use: Use as guardrail: confidence/display accuracy must not be treated as profitability or edge.

title: Comparing two methods for testing efficiency
usable_claim: Under strong-form betting-market efficiency, normalized model probabilities should not systematically differ from bookmaker-implied probabilities.
vmax_use: Market layer should compare fair model probabilities against bookmaker probabilities and treat large deviations as signals requiring evidence.

title: Betting market efficiency and overround allocation methods
usable_claim: Different margin-removal methods allocate overround differently; multiplicative, additive, power, and Shin approaches may produce materially different fair probabilities.
vmax_use: Do not hard-code one de-vig method as truth; record method and compare sensitivity.

title: Dixon-Coles & Poisson Models in Football Prediction
usable_claim: Football scores can be modeled through team attack/defence rates with Poisson grids; Dixon-Coles adjusts low-score dependence.
vmax_use: Score Distribution Layer should produce full score grids, not just single-score narratives.

title: Bayesian vs LLM Football Prediction
usable_claim: Extended xG can feed Poisson lambdas; low-score correction such as Dixon-Coles rho and renormalisation keep the score grid coherent.
vmax_use: AI residuals should explain or adjust inputs, while probability trunk stays mathematical and normalized.

title: Lineups, team news, injuries and odds movement in football betting
usable_claim: Lineups, injuries and team news can move prices near close; stale pre-match information creates prediction risk.
vmax_use: Forward ledger must lock prediction time and record whether lineup/news was known before kick-off.

title: AI Engineer Path - prediction prompt leakage/evaluation references
usable_claim: AI prediction workflows are vulnerable to leakage and overfit; forward-only evaluation beats retrospective prompt tuning.
vmax_use: Ban post-result prompt rewriting and sandbox fabrication; use SHA-locked blind ledgers.
